comments.author: @GlobeDiscovery-NY
comments.text: The famous colorful colonial buildings in Willemstad actually have a quirky history. Back in the 1800s, a governor claimed the intense Caribbean sun reflecting off the white walls gave him severe migraines, so he passed a law requiring all buildings to be painted in any color except white. It turns out he owned a share in the only local paint company, but that decree is why the island is so vibrant today.

replies.author: @TheGlobalTruth04
replies.text: What a fun bit of history! 😄 True or not, those colorful streets have become one of Willemstad's most iconic features. Thanks for sharing this interesting story! 🌈

comments.author: @GlobalRealLifeDocumentary
comments.text: Curaçao has some of the clearest turquoise water, colorful streets, and a fascinating mix of Caribbean and European culture—yet so many people have never even heard of it. 🇨🇼 After watching this documentary, would you choose Curaçao over the Bahamas or Aruba for your next island vacation? Why or why not?
